title: Sweet-Crisp Kenyan Prawns Stir-fry
description: A low-fat-friendly solo-dinner dish featuring raw tiger prawns in a sweet and crispy sauce.
instructions: Prepare the raw prawns by removing any shells and membranes. Rinse them under cold water.
In a mixing bowl, combine the low-fat soy sauce, sweet corn starch, and soya milk to create the sauce base.
Place the prawns in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Brush each shrimp lightly with oil for crispiness.
Broil the prawns at high heat until they turn golden brown, about 2 minutes per side. Remove from heat and set aside.
Gently stir-fry the crispy tapioca fingers in a non-stick pan over medium heat to ensure they are not burnt, adding them after the prawns have been broiled.
Once both prawns and tapioca fingers are ready, add them back into the mixing bowl with sauce base. Mix well until all prawns are coated evenly with the sauce.
Toss in the chopped mint leaves for a fresh flavor boost before serving.
Serve hot as an appetizer or main course with steamed rice or vegetables.
